english: Lahmacun with Kofta and Shish Tawook

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for lahmacun or a lahmacun platter. FOODON_03315595 (flatbread food product) is used as the closest defensible anchor for the flatbread component. The composite platter nature (flatbread + grilled meats) is not captured by a single FoodOn term.
ontology.foodex2_description: Composite dishes — meat-based mixed dish with bread
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FMT (composite meat-based mixed dish with bread) is the closest defensible category for a platter combining spiced minced-meat flatbreads with grilled kebab items. No exact FoodEx2 code exists for lahmacun specifically.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT clinical food concept found for lahmacun or Turkish flatbread platter. SNOMED CT is not applicable for this composite dish record.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard platter serving is approximately 800 g, designed to be shared among 2–3 people, yielding roughly 267–400 g per person.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Composite estimate derived from: Lamb, ground, raw (FDC ID 174352); Chicken, broilers or fryers, meat only, raw (FDC ID 171477); Bread, pita, white, unenriched (FDC ID 174924); Beef/lamb kofta kebab, composite (FDC ID 168930 analogous)
nutrient_source.organization: U.S. Department of Agriculture, Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single USDA FoodData Central entry exists for a lahmacun platter. Nutrient values were estimated by weighted-component modelling: approximately 40% thin wheat flatbread (pita analogue), 30% minced lamb (raw ground lamb, cooked adjustment), 20% minced/grilled chicken (broiler meat), and 10% vegetables and spices. The input record's per-100g values (energy 225 kcal, protein 15 g, fat 11.25 g, carbohydrate 15 g) are consistent with this composite model and have been retained with minor rounding. Micronutrient values (calcium 18.75 mg, iron 1.88 mg, cholesterol 50 mg, saturated fat 4.38 g, trans fat 0.06 g, vitamin C 5 mg) are also consistent with the USDA component data and have been retained. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, potassium, and several vitamins are set to -1 as detailed fatty acid and micronutrient breakdowns for this composite preparation are not available in a single approved source entry.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281–2283. — Pita bread, white (GI 57, glucose reference) used as analogous estimate for lahmacun flatbread base.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for lahmacun or a lahmacun platter. White pita bread (GI 57, medium) is used as the closest analogous food from the University of Sydney GI Database, as lahmacun uses a similar thin wheat-flour dough base. The high protein content (15 g/100g) from the meat toppings is expected to blunt the glycaemic response relative to plain pita, so GI 57 is a conservative upper-bound estimate. Glycaemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (57 × 60 g carbohydrate in 400 g per-person serving) / 100 = 34.2. Per-person serving assumed at 400 g (half of 800 g platter for 2 people).

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content at 312.5 mg/100g; estimated 1,250 mg sodium per 400 g per-person serving approaches 54% of the 2,300 mg daily limit.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit their portion to no more than 200–250 g and request reduced salt during preparation, avoiding additional high-sodium condiments such as soy sauce or chilli sauce at the table.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate carbohydrate load (15 g/100g) from the wheat flour flatbread base with a medium estimated GI of 57; high protein (15 g/100g) partially attenuates postprandial glucose rise.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with type 2 diabetes should limit their portion to 200–250 g, pair the platter with a fibre-rich side salad to lower the overall glycaemic response, and monitor blood glucose after consumption given the wheat-flour base.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat at 4.38 g/100g and cholesterol at 50 mg/100g from lamb and chicken; a 400 g per-person serving delivers approximately 17.5 g saturated fat, exceeding the recommended daily limit of 20 g for most adults.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit their portion to 150–200 g, favour the chicken lahmacun and shish tawook components over the lamb kofta to reduce saturated fat intake, and avoid adding cream-based sauces.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Minced lamb is a red meat and a dietary source of arachidonic acid (omega-6), which amplifies pro-inflammatory eicosanoid pathways relevant to rheumatoid arthritis.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid the lamb lahmacun and lamb kofta components and request a chicken-only version; those with nightshade sensitivity should also note that tomatoes and bell peppers are present in the topping.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Lahmacun Platter is a generous shared platter served at Turkish and Middle Eastern restaurants in Singapore, featuring two lamb lahmacun, two chicken lahmacun, lamb kofta, and shish tawook (grilled chicken skewers). Lahmacun — sometimes called 'Turkish pizza' — is a thin, crispy wheat-flour flatbread topped with finely minced and spiced meat (lamb or chicken), tomato, onion, bell pepper, garlic, parsley, cumin, and paprika, then baked at high heat until the edges are slightly charred and the base is crisp. The kofta are hand-shaped minced lamb patties seasoned with onion, cumin, and herbs, grilled over charcoal or a gas grill. The shish tawook are marinated chicken cubes grilled on skewers. The platter is typically served with lemon wedges, fresh parsley, and a side of pickled vegetables or salad. Nutritionally, the platter provides approximately 225 kcal, 15 g protein, 11.25 g fat, and 15 g carbohydrate per 100 g, making it a moderate-energy, high-protein dish. Sodium is 312.5 mg/100g, which is borderline for hypertension management. The dish is halal-certified in most Singapore outlets. Clinically, the wheat-flour base contributes a medium glycaemic index (estimated GI 57 by analogy with white pita bread), while the high protein content partially attenuates postprandial glucose excursions. Saturated fat from lamb (4.38 g/100g) warrants attention for cardiovascular and rheumatoid arthritis patients. The dish is culturally rooted in Turkish cuisine and is popular in Singapore's Middle Eastern restaurant scene.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a lahmacun platter?
What is the difference between lahmacun and pizza?
Is lahmacun the same as Turkish pizza?
Can I eat lahmacun if I have high blood pressure?
How much protein is in lahmacun?
Is lahmacun suitable for weight loss?

confidence_reason: No single approved primary source (USDA FoodData Central, HPB Singapore, ICMR-NIN, or IMR Malaysia MyFCD) contains a direct entry for lahmacun platter. Nutrient values were estimated by weighted-component modelling using USDA FoodData Central entries for ground lamb, chicken breast, and white pita bread as component analogues. The input record's per-100g values are consistent with this model and have been retained. GI is estimated by analogy with white pita bread from the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are composite estimates derived from USDA FoodData Central component entries (ground lamb FDC 174352, chicken broiler meat FDC 171477, white pita bread FDC 174924) weighted approximately 30% lamb, 20% chicken, 40% flatbread, 10% vegetables/spices. The input record values are consistent with this model and have been retained. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid profiles, full micronutrient breakdowns, and moisture data for this composite Turkish flatbread-and-grilled-meat platter are not available as a single entry in USDA FoodData Central, HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, or IMR Malaysia MyFCD as of April 2026. 3. GI of 57 is an analogous estimate based on white pita bread (University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care); no direct GI measurement for lahmacun was found in the approved GI database. The high protein content (15 g/100g) is expected to lower the effective glycaemic response below this estimate. 4. The dish is listed as halal by the GrabFood source; this has been retained as a dietary flag. 5. The original input record used the spelling 'Lahmacun Plater' (single 't'); the canonical English food name has been corrected to 'Lahmacun Platter'. 6. Multilingual names (Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) have been added per Project Veritas v2 rules for dishes served in Singapore, reflecting the Turkish-origin dish name in each language. 7. The original source URL (https://foodo.sg/) in the input record does not correspond to the HPB Singapore Foodo nutrient database and has not been used as a nutrient source. USDA FoodData Central has been used instead as the approved secondary global source.
